title: Magnificently Roasted Filipino Lamb Halo Halo
description: A salty and creamy Filipino dish featuring lamb shanks, perfect for a weeknight dinner, this recipe is a twist on the traditional halo halo dessert, instead using roasted lamb and vegetables in a savory broth, served over a bed of rice and topped with a creamy sauce and various garnishes.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Season the lamb shanks with salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shanks until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Remove the lamb from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onions to the pot and cook until they are softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic, fish sauce, soy sauce, and rice vinegar to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Return the lamb to the pot and add enough water to cover the lamb.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Roast the lamb for 2 1/2 hours, or until it is tender and falling off the bone.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the creamy sauce by whisking together the heavy cream and a pinch of salt and pepper in a bowl.
To serve, place a scoop of warm rice in a bowl, then add a lamb shank, some of the roasted onions and garlic, and a spoonful of the creamy sauce.
